title: iris missouriensis var. arizonica
intro: Iris missouriensis var. arizonica, also known as the Arizona iris, is a perennial plant that belongs to the iris family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's rhizomes and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Arizona iris is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
